overview: Rakdos Prowess is a fast, spell-slinging aggro deck that leverages cheap noncreature spells to repeatedly trigger the prowess mechanic. By cycling through low-cost creatures and instant-speed interactions, it aims to overwhelm opponents before they can establish a stable board state. The deck thrives on efficient resource usage and graveyard recursion to maintain relentless pressure.
win_conditions: Combat damage from repeatedly buffed prowess creatures
Graveyard recursion of Slickshot Show-Off to close out games
Leveraging Emberheart Challenger's death trigger to create a large threat

keep_criteria: Hands with a turn-one prowess creature and at least one noncreature spell
Curves that guarantee plays on turns two and three
Removal or interaction to handle early threats
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one turn-1 or turn-2 threat (Slickshot Show-Off, Monastery Swiftspear, Emberheart Challenger), and cheap spells/removal (Fatal Push, Lightning Strike, Go Blank).
Prioritize hands that can cast a spell on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking noncreature spells to trigger prowess
Stuck on three-drops or higher without early interaction
Multiple high-cost creatures with no cheap spells
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, only expensive threats, or no way to trigger Prowess early.

early_game: Deploy Monastery Swiftspear immediately and cast cheap instants or sorceries to buff it
Prioritize land drops and spell density to maximize prowess triggers
Use early removal to clear blockers and keep damage on track
Turn 1: Play a basic land, cast a cheap spell (Fatal Push, Lightning Strike, or Slickshot Show-Off if mana allows).
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast another spell, and drop a mid-tier threat like Emberheart Challenger or Slickshot.
Sequence lands to ensure access to double red/black for Swiftspear and Emberheart, but don't flood with red if you need black for removal.
mid_game: Introduce Slickshot Show-Off and Emberheart Challenger to apply wider pressure
Cast noncreature spells before combat to maximize prowess buffs
Begin recycling Slickshot from the graveyard to maintain board presence
Treat your hand as a resource to trigger Prowess
Use removal only when necessary to preserve threats.
Keep an eye on your graveyard for Fatal Push targets and Bloodtithe Harvester value.
Commit threats when the opponent has no sweepers or targeted removal.
Hold back Monastery Swiftspear until it can swing for lethal or trade favorably.
late_game: Focus on finishing the game with accumulated prowess counters
Use Emberheart Challenger's death trigger to create a final threat
Maintain spell density to outvalue slower decks and close out matches
Monastery Swiftspear is your primary win condition.
Once it reaches 4/4 or larger, swing aggressively.
Emberheart Challenger can also close games if it survives combat or trades into a key blocker.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal, no sweepers, and you have Prowess triggers ready to scale your attackers.
Also commit when you can race their win condition or force a combat math advantage.

key_cards.name: Monastery Swiftspear
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one and trigger prowess with every noncreature spell to quickly become a relevant threat

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Cast from hand or graveyard to maintain board presence and trigger prowess repeatedly

key_cards.name: Emberheart Challenger
key_cards.notes: Use as a sacrifice outlet or let it deal combat damage to place a +1/+1 counter on another creature for a game-ending push

sequencing_tips: Cast noncreature spells before declaring attackers to maximize prowess buffs
Hold removal in hand to respond to opponent's key threats or blockers
Time Emberheart Challenger's death trigger to leave a counter on your largest creature
Prioritize casting spells that trigger prowess over drawing cards when ahead on damage
Prowess rewards spell efficiency, so don’t fear casting seemingly 'bad' spells if they trigger Prowess.
Watch your graveyard for Fatal Push and Harvester value.
Don’t overcommit to the board without considering sweepers.
Practice combat math with Swiftspear to avoid misplays.
Remember that Prowess triggers on any instant, sorcery, or activated ability, so use creature abilities and discard effects to your advantage.

matchups.advice: Push damage aggressively before they stabilize with sweepers or card draw
Race efficiently by using removal on their biggest threats and triggering prowess to outpace them
Apply constant pressure with cheap spells and prowess triggers to prevent them from developing value
Disrupt their setup with early interaction and close out quickly before they assemble their win condition

Cast multiple cheap spells to force counters, then drop a threat. Use instant-speed threats or sacrifice outlets to bypass counters. Sometimes accepting a counter is fine if you maintain pressure and keep triggering Prowess.

Race them aggressively. They’re slow early, so don’t let them stabilize. Use discard and removal to disrupt their mana development and force them to spend resources on interaction instead of acceleration.

Win through resource efficiency and sequencing. Use discard to disrupt their game plan. Avoid overextending; value trades and win by out-scaling or out-removing. Prioritize keeping your Swiftspear alive until it can swing for lethal.

common_threats: Board wipes that clear prowess counters
Card draw engines that outpace the deck
Wide creature spreads that block damage
Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Wrath of God), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Doom Blade), and counterspells.
Note when the opponent taps out for a single spell—that’s your window to drop a threat.
Track graveyard contents for Fatal Push and Harvester synergies.
